title: Duplicate layers in Image inspect information build using podman build
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: **kind bug**


**Description**
I found this issue with this [particular](https://github.com/couchbase/docker/tree/master/enterprise/couchbase-server/4.0.0) image build using `podman build`. It shows same layer multiple times when `podman inspect` is done on image.

It is easily reproducible. I verified with `docker build` and there is no issue with that.


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. git clone https://github.com/couchbase/docker 

2. cd docker/enterprise/couchbase-server/4.0.0

3. sudo podman  build --no-cache -f Dockerfile -t layers .

4. sudo podman inspect localhost/layers

**Describe the results you received:**
```
.
. <snip>
.
           }
        },
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ sudo podman version 
[sudo] password for kunal:        
Version:       0.10.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.10
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
On Ubuntu, physical machine.

comments.body:
@kunalkushwaha Seems like this is probably an issue in Buildah code, though I suppose it could be an issue with how Podman displays the image's layers...

@TomSweeneyRedHat WDYT?
@mheon Shall I report this in buildah repository?
@kunalkushwaha Probably a good idea, though we should keep this open until we know for sure it's not a display issue on our side
I think it probably ought to be logged against Buildah.  I've just opened https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1151.  @mheon I'll let you make the call on closing this one now or later.
I'll keep this open in case it turns out to be Podman, not buildah - if so, just ping us back on this bug and we'll start working on it over here
@kunalkushwaha and @mheon I'm seeing the same issue using only Buildah.  Given that I'm going to close this issue and we'll continue on with https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1151
SGTM - once you're done over there we'll vendor the fix back in.
